question_text: When you try to apply classroom knowledge in real situations, how confident do you usually feel?
How often do you actively practice skills outside regular classroom learning?

After seeing others perform well in coding rounds, how discouraged do you usually feel?
How stressful do extreme time limits feel during exams, coding rounds, or interviews?
How strongly do classroom or peer discussions make you feel conceptually behind?
How often do you feel that classroom learning is not directly useful for real job tasks?
How often do you try to apply classroom concepts in real mini-projects or internships?
How often do you feel unsure of your answers during coding or technical tests?
During coding problems, how often do you feel overwhelmed by complexity?
How stressed do you feel when trying to balance multiple skills (coding, projects, communication, etc.)?
How often do students around you actively discuss skills like coding, communication, or projects?
How common is it for students to work on real projects outside of classroom requirements?
How much do students compete in skills (coding, design, speaking) rather than just marks?
How easy is it for you to identify opportunities for growth beyond classroom learning?
How often do you interact with faculty or mentors beyond classroom teaching?
How often do you feel classroom discussions help you clarify doubts effectively?
How much pressure do you feel to ensure every classroom performs equally well in learning outcomes?
How often do you feel that one classroom performs significantly better or worse than others under your instruction?
When classroom conditions differ, how effectively can you maintain similar learning outcomes?

Even if classroom performance varies now, how hopeful are you that you can achieve consistent learning quality in the future?

How much do classroom notes help you understand concepts on your own later?

How often do you use external resources (videos, notes, internet) to understand classroom topics better?
How strongly do you believe classroom learning should focus on understanding rather than exams?
How well do you feel your classroom learning translates into real-world job skills?
How frustrated do you feel when classroom concepts dont seem useful in real life?
How often do you feel classroom learning does not help in practical problem solving?
How different is classroom learning from real workplace tasks?
How often do you rely on online courses instead of classroom teaching for skill development?
How confident are you in applying classroom concepts to real projects?
How often do classroom learning experiences feel relevant to real-world skills and competencies?
How much does the current curriculum fail to motivate you to actively explore beyond classroom content?
How strongly do you feel that students around you are also disengaged from classroom learning?
How often do you rely on external sources (YouTube, notes, etc.) because classroom teaching feels insufficient?
How likely are you to skip deeper learning because classroom delivery does not motivate you?
How often do you actively revise or practice topics because classroom delivery alone is not enough?

How ready are you to actively work on improving your classroom concentration and attention span?
If you received one practical technique today to improve classroom focus, how likely are you to use it seriously?
How well are you able to maintain attention throughout a full classroom lecture?
How often do you feel mentally tired or bored during classroom lectures?
How strongly do external distractions affect your ability to focus in a classroom environment?
How often do you lose track of what the teacher is explaining during lectures?
How confident are you that you can improve your classroom attention span over time?
How much pressure do you feel to choose a conventional career path (e.g., engineering, medicine, government jobs) even if it may not match your interests?
After failure, how difficult is it to understand teacher feedback?
When a teacher explains a concept once, how often do you feel confused afterward?
How often do you need additional self-study beyond classroom teaching to understand basic concepts?
When you dont get immediate answers, how patient are you in figuring things out yourself?
Do you feel nervous when thinking about interviews or HR rounds?
How frequently do you stick to daily practice goals for coding or interview topics?
If faced with a real-world issue (workplace, finance, or daily life), how ready would you feel to solve it effectively?
In hackathons or coding problems, how quickly do you lose direction?
How much does academic pressure reduce your time for skill-building or coding practice?
During coding contests, how emotionally stable do you feel when solving hard problems?
How stressed do you feel during live coding contests?
How often do you use breathing/mental techniques during coding stress?
Do you believe emotional control is as important as coding skill in contests?
How hopeful are you that you can improve emotional stability in coding contests?

During interviews or coding tests, how confident are you in making instant decisions without overthinking?
During coding contests or exams, how often do you get stuck on one question for too long?
How confident are you about pursuing a career that is not considered traditional (like engineering or government jobs)?
How likely are you to explore startup or freelance opportunities during college?
If you had a chance to try a startup internship, how likely are you to take it?
How often do you actively experiment or practice coding/building while learning theory?
How confident are you in solving coding or academic problems without relying on tutorials or step-by-step guides?
How often do you struggle to apply classroom concepts in real situations?
How much does your internship experience help you solve placement-style coding/interview problems?
Do you feel emotionally overwhelmed when balancing multiple placement tasks like coding, aptitude, and interviews?
How confident do you feel about handling anxiety during HR interview rounds?
How often do you feel nervous before or during HR interviews?
Do you feel anxious when answering personal or behavioral questions in HR rounds?
Compared to your peers, how confident do you feel in HR interviews?
If you attend an HR round tomorrow, how prepared would you feel to stay calm and answer confidently?
How stressful do HR interviews feel to you overall?
How often do you experience overthinking before HR interviews?
How much does anxiety affect your ability to express yourself clearly in HR rounds?

After completing an HR interview, how quickly do you recover from nervousness or stress?
How confident are you that you can eventually manage HR interview anxiety effectively?
How ready are you to actively work on reducing your anxiety during HR interviews?
If guided practice was available, how willing would you be to participate in mock HR interviews?
How strongly do you believe confidence in HR interviews can be improved through practice?
If given one strategy to reduce HR anxiety, how likely are you to apply it consistently?
Even if HR interviews feel stressful now, how hopeful are you that you can become confident over time?
